title: CI scaffold: Biolink/SHACL + provenance-completeness + allow-list linter
context: The project's 100% provenance guarantee and license compliance must be enforced mechanically by CI, not by human review alone. This CI scaffold implements three gates: (1) Biolink/SHACL structural validation of graph data, (2) provenance-completeness check (every assertion must carry all mandatory provenance fields), and (3) allow-list linter (any assertion referencing a source not marked 'approved' in sources/allowlist.yml fails). Depends on the schema (oncogene-kg-schema-001) and provenance model (oncogene-kg-prov-001).
objective: Implement a CI pipeline (GitHub Actions) that runs Biolink/SHACL validation, rejects any assertion lacking complete resolvable provenance, and rejects any assertion referencing a source not approved in the allow-list.
acceptanceCriteria: CI fails on any assertion in the graph data lacking resolvable provenance (all mandatory fields per oncogene-kg-prov-001).
CI runs Biolink/SHACL validation and fails on structural violations.
CI rejects data referencing a source not marked 'approved' in sources/allowlist.yml.
All three gates run on every PR touching graph data files.
CI passes on the initial empty/stub graph state.
A brief CI README documents how to run the checks locally.
output: .github/workflows/ci.yml implementing Biolink/SHACL validation, provenance-completeness, and allow-list linting gates, committed via PR.
